texto: You're beautiful, but you're empty...One couldn't die for you. Of course, an ordinary passerby would think my rose looked just like you. But my rose, all on her own, is more important than all of you together, since she's the one I've watered. Since she's the one I put under glass, since she's the one I sheltered behind the screen. Since she's the one for whom I killed the caterpillars (except the two or three butterflies). Since she's the one I listened to when she complained, or when she boasted, or even sometimes when she said nothing at all. Since she's my rose.

texto: Na vida, não existem soluções. Existem forças em marcha: é preciso criá-las e, então, a elas seguem-se as soluções.
explicacao: De Antoine de Saint-Exupéry, aviador e escritor francês. Contraria a ideia de soluções prontas: primeiro é preciso criar forças, energia ou movimento; só então as respostas aparecem.
